entities.name: RICE
entities.description: The primary crop being cultivated using different systems.

entities.name: PUDDLED SYSTEM OF RICE CULTIVATION
entities.description: A conventional, resource-intensive rice cultivation method.

entities.name: DIRECT SEEDED RICE
entities.description: An alternative rice cultivation system that avoids puddling.

entities.name: BREEDING
entities.description: The process of developing improved crop varieties for DSR.

entities.name: AGRONOMIC APPROACHES
entities.description: Management practices used to sustain DSR and harness resources.

entities.name: CROP ESTABLISHMENT
entities.description: The initial phase of crop growth in the field, often poor in DSR.

entities.name: GERMINATION
entities.description: The process of seed sprouting, which affects crop establishment.

entities.name: DSR-ADAPTED VARIETIES
entities.description: Rice varieties specifically bred or selected for the DSR system.

entities.name: WEED
entities.description: Unwanted plants that cause high incidence constraints in DSR.

entities.name: NEMATODE
entities.description: Parasitic worms that cause high incidence constraints in DSR.

entities.name: MICRONUTRIENT DEFICIENCY
entities.description: A condition where essential micronutrients are lacking, constraining DSR.

entities.name: MOLECULAR CHARACTERIZATION
entities.description: The use of molecular tools to identify strains for DSR.

entities.name: MECHANIZATION
entities.description: The application of machinery to improve DSR efficiency.

entities.name: WATER-NUTRIENT-WEED MANAGEMENT
entities.description: Integrated strategies to manage resources and pests in DSR.

entities.name: GENES/QTL
entities.description: Genetic markers or quantitative trait loci required for DSR traits.

entities.name: DSR-ADAPTED BREEDING LINES
entities.description: Genetic lines developed to be suitable for DSR.

entities.name: POLICIES
entities.description: Regulatory or strategic frameworks needed to scale up DSR adoption.
